content.full_text:
May 31, 1990

Mrs. John Wallach:

Dear Sir:
    In regards to the check "Riverside Townway" now terminated, we received from the State Comptroller's Division turned in for the amount of 378.—
    Can I to asper resper the checking Account- deposit this make check to an outstanding debt? Or can I purchase a cassier check and play an outstanding debt?
    Need to get this matter taken care of as soon as possible.

Due P.C. Terminated 12-89-
Also will need to refill any forms?

                                        Mrs. E. Dashnawski
                                        2523 Adams Ave
                                        Emmett Ca 92304
                                        Former Foreman
                                        Riverside Townway

sections.question: In regards to the check "Riverside Tomorrow" now terminated, have received from the State Compensation Insurance Fund for the amount of $378. Am I to reopen the checking account - deposit then make check to an outstanding debt? Or can I purchase a cashier check and pay an outstanding debt? Need to get this matter taken care of as soon as possible. Our PAC terminated 12-89. Also do I need to resile any forms?
sections.question_synthetic: The former treasurer of a terminated PAC (Riverside Tomorrow, terminated 12/89) seeks guidance on how to handle a $378 check received from the State Compensation Insurance Fund, specifically whether she should reopen the PAC's checking account or use alternative payment methods to settle outstanding debts, and whether any forms need to be filed.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry letter, not an FPPC response. No formal question and conclusion sections are present. The document contains a practical question about PAC termination procedures and fund handling. Writer appears to have some grammatical uncertainties ("resile" may be a misspelling). No FPPC letterhead or response is present.
